evals.prompt: Run a database audit on src/data/users.repo.ts. Score its query patterns and security, and tell me what's failing.
evals.expected_output: The audit catches the planted DB anti-patterns in src/data/users.repo.ts: (DB1) an N+1 where loadUsers awaits db.user.findUnique sequentially inside a for-loop (one query per id); (DB12) a SQL injection where searchByEmail concatenates the email into a $queryRawUnsafe string; and (DB1) an unbounded db.order.findMany with no take in allOrders. Each is reported at its file:line. Because a critical gate trips (DB1=0 N+1 in a hot path, and/or DB12=0 SQL injection), the grade is FAIL. The audit writes its report to zuvo/audits/ and modifies no source file.
assertions: The audit records a DB1 N+1 finding against loadUsers in src/data/users.repo.ts — db.user.findUnique is awaited sequentially inside the for-loop, one query per id — citing its file:line.

The audit records the DB12 SQL-injection finding (email concatenated into the $queryRawUnsafe string in searchByEmail) and the DB1 unbounded db.order.findMany with no take in allOrders, at their file:line locations.
The audit records a FAIL grade via a tripped critical gate (DB1=0 N+1 or DB12=0 SQL injection) and writes the db-audit report to zuvo/audits/ without modifying any source file.

evals.prompt: Run a database audit on src/data/users.repo.ts and give me its grade.
evals.expected_output: The audit shows false-positive discipline: it does NOT flag an N+1 (loadUsers issues a single batched db.user.findMany with an `in` filter, not a per-id loop), does NOT flag a SQL injection (searchByEmail uses a tagged-template $queryRaw that binds the email as a parameter, not string concatenation), and does NOT flag an unbounded query (recentOrders has an explicit clamped take). It gives the file a passing grade with the critical gates (DB1/DB12) not tripped, and writes the db-audit report to zuvo/audits/ — modifying no source file. Defenses recognized, not hallucinated findings.
assertions: The audit records no DB1 N+1 finding against loadUsers in src/data/users.repo.ts — the single batched db.user.findMany with an `in` filter is recognized as one query, not a per-id loop.

The audit records no DB12 SQL-injection finding against searchByEmail (the tagged-template $queryRaw binds the email as a parameter) and no unbounded-query finding against recentOrders (explicit clamped take) — the parameterized query and bounded pagination are recognized as mitigated.
The audit records a passing grade with the critical gates (DB1/DB12) not tripped, and writes the db-audit report to zuvo/audits/ without modifying any source file.
